title: राधा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री राधा स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
तप्तकाञ्चनगौराङ्गीं रत्नाभरणभूषिताम्।
कृष्णप्रेमपरां देवीं राधिकां चिन्तयाम्यहम्॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
नमस्ते राधिके देवि गोलोकेश्वरि पूजिते।
कृष्णकान्ते जगन्मातर्नमस्ते नमस्ते नमः॥ १॥

त्वं हि राधा श्रीकृष्णस्य प्राणाधिका प्रिया सदा।
त्वया विना न गोविन्दस्त्वं विना स न राजते॥ २॥

वृन्दावनविहारिण्यां रासलीलाविलासिनी।
गोपिकानां शिरोरत्नं राधे त्वां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ३॥

बरसानेश्वरी तन्वि वृषभानुसुतामते।
कीर्तिदानन्दकन्ये त्वां राधे त्वां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

कुञ्जविहारिणि राधे निकुञ्जनायिके शुभे।
रसिकेश्वरि सर्वज्ञे राधे त्वां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

गोवर्धनधरप्रिये गोकुलानन्ददायिनि।
यमुनातीरवासिन्यां राधे त्वां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ६॥

आह्लादिनीशक्तिरूपे कृष्णप्रेमस्वरूपिणि।
गोलोकवासिनि राधे राधे त्वां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

ललिताविशाखासख्यां श्रीदामसुबलप्रिये।
चन्द्रावलीविजयिन्यां राधे त्वां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ८॥

मानिनि मानदे राधे प्रेमवत्यां कृपामयि।
नवयौवनसम्पन्ने राधे त्वां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ९॥

श्रीकृष्णवक्षःस्थलवासिनि शक्ते।
सर्वभक्तजनवत्सले माते।
प्रेमभक्तिवरदे कृपापूर्णे।
राधिके तव चरणं प्रपद्ये॥ १०॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
राधास्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
कृष्णप्रेमं लभेत् सोऽपि राधामाधवयोः पदम्॥

॥ इति श्री राधा स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Radha Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Tapta-kanchana-gaurangi Ratna-abharana-bhushitam.
Krishna-prema-param Devim Radhikam Chintayamyaham.

|| Stotram ||
Namaste Radhike Devi Golokeshwari Pujite.
Krishna-kante Jagan-matar-namaste Namaste Namah. || 1 ||

Tvam Hi Radha Shri-Krishnasya Prana-adhika Priya Sada.
Tvaya Vina Na Govindas-tvam Vina Sa Na Rajate. || 2 ||

Vrindavana-viharinya Rasa-lila-vilasini.
Gopikanam Shiro-ratnam Radhe Tvam Pranamamyaham. || 3 ||

Barasaneshwari Tanvi Vrishabhanusuta-amate.
Kirti-da-ananda-kanye Tvam Radhe Tvam Pranamamyaham. || 4 ||

Kunja-viharini Radhe Nikunja-nayike Shubhe.
Rasikeshwari Sarvajne Radhe Tvam Pranamamyaham. || 5 ||

Govardhana-dhara-priye Gokula-ananda-dayini.
Yamuna-tira-vasinya Radhe Tvam Pranamamyaham. || 6 ||

Ahladini-shakti-rupe Krishna-prema-svarupini.
Goloka-vasini Radhe Radhe Tvam Pranamamyaham. || 7 ||

Lalita-vishakha-sakhyam Shridama-subala-priye.
Chandravali-vijayinyam Radhe Tvam Pranamamyaham. || 8 ||

Manini Manade Radhe Prema-vatyam Kripaamayi.
Nava-yauvana-sampanne Radhe Tvam Pranamamyaham. || 9 ||

Shri-Krishna-vakshah-sthala-vasini Shakte.
Sarva-bhakta-jana-vatsale Mate.
Prema-bhakti-varade Kripa-purne.
Radhike Tava Charanam Prapadye. || 10 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Radha-stotram-idam Punyam Yah Pathed-bhakti-samyutah.
Krishna-premam Labhet So'pi Radha-madhavayoh Padam.

|| Iti Shri Radha Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
तप्त सोने के समान गौर अंगों वाली, रत्नाभरणों से सुशोभित, कृष्णप्रेम में परायण देवी राधिका का ध्यान करता हूँ।

हे राधिके देवी! गोलोक की ईश्वरी! कृष्णकान्ते! जगन्माता! आपको बारम्बार नमस्कार। आप श्रीकृष्ण की प्राणों से भी अधिक प्रिया हैं, आपके बिना गोविन्द शोभायमान नहीं होते।

वृन्दावन में विहार करने वाली, रासलीला में विलासिनी, गोपिकाओं में शिरोरत्न! बरसाने की ईश्वरी, वृषभानु की पुत्री, कीर्तिदा-आनन्द की कन्या! कुञ्ज विहारिणी, निकुञ्ज नायिका, रसिकेश्वरी!

आह्लादिनी शक्तिस्वरूपा, कृष्णप्रेमस्वरूपिणी, गोलोकवासिनी! ललिता-विशाखा की सखी, मानिनी, प्रेमवती, कृपामयी!

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है, वह कृष्णप्रेम प्राप्त करता है और राधा-माधव का पद पाता है।
benefits: Invokes krishna-prema (divine love for lord krishna)
Invokes the blessing of the supreme abode of radha-madhav
Connects the devotee with Radha's ahladini shakti (bliss power)
Deepens devotional sentiment and purifies the heart
Especially powerful on Radha Ashtami, Holi, and Sharad Purnima
Invokes the grace and protection of shri radha rani
description: Dedicated to Goddess Radha, the Radha Stotram (राधा स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. Attributed to Narada Pancharatra, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant Krishna-prema (divine love for Lord Krishna) as well as deepen devotional sentiment and purify the heart. Additionally, it is known to bestow the supreme abode of Radha-Madhav. It is especially recommended on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Sharad Purnima, Janmashtami, and Holi for maximum spiritual benefit. At 10 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Radha Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Radha Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Devotional prayer to Shri Radha for divine love, Krishna-prema, and spiritual bliss.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Radha Stotram and where is it preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Radha Stotram (राधा स्तोत्रम्) is a beautiful Sanskrit devotional hymn dedicated to Sri Radha — the supreme consort of Lord Krishna and the very ahladini shakti (bliss-power) of the Para Brahman. The stotra is preserved in the classical Narada Pancharatra tradition (one of the foundational Pancharatra texts of Vaishnava theology, attributed to Devarshi Narada). The hymn opens with a dhyana visualising Radha as tapta-kanchana-gauranga (golden-bodied like molten gold), ratna-abharana-bhushita (adorned with gem-ornaments) and Krishna-prema-para (absorbed in Krishna-prema). It then unfolds through ten verses praising her as the Goloka-eshwari, Krishna-kante, jagan-mata, the daughter of Vrishabhanu and Kirtida, the queen of the rasa-lila, the leader of the gopis, and the embodiment of the ahladini-shakti, culminating in a phalashruti promising Krishna-prema and the supreme abode of Radha-Madhava. (Sources: Narada Pancharatra; Vaishnava bhakti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the principal names and forms of Sri Radha invoked in the stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Across its ten verses the Radha Stotram invokes virtually every major name and aspect of Sri Radha. Verse 1: Radhike Devi, Goloka-eshvari (queen of Goloka), Krishna-kante (beloved of Krishna), Jagan-mata (Mother of the Universe). Verse 2: Krishna-prana-adhika-priya (dearer than Krishna's own life). Verse 3: Vrindavana-viharini (delighter in Vrindavan), Rasa-lila-vilasini, Gopikanam shiro-ratnam (crest-jewel of the gopis). Verse 4: Barsaneshvari (queen of Barsana), Vrishabhanu-suta (daughter of Vrishabhanu), Kirti-da-ananda-kanya (joyful daughter of Kirtida). Verse 5: Kunja-viharini, Nikunja-nayika, Rasikeshvari. Verse 6: Govardhana-dhara-priye, Yamuna-tira-vasini. Verse 7: Ahladini-shakti-rupa, Krishna-prema-svarupini, Goloka-vasini. Verse 8: friend of Lalita and Vishakha, dear to Shridama and Subala. Verse 9: Manini, prema-vati, kripa-mayi, nava-yauvana-sampanne. Verse 10: dwelling on Krishna's chest, prema-bhakti-varada. (Sources: Radha Stotram complete.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Sri Radha called the 'Ahladini Shakti' of Krishna?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Radha is called the Ahladini Shakti — the bliss-giving power — of Lord Krishna in the classical Vaishnava theology articulated in the Brahma Vaivarta Purana, Narada Pancharatra and the writings of the six Goswamis of Vrindavan. The Para Brahman is described as having three principal shaktis: sat (existence/Sandhini), chit (consciousness/Samvit) and ananda (bliss/Ahladini). Of these the supreme is Ahladini — the bliss-power — for it is through Ahladini that Krishna himself enjoys his own ananda-svarupa and that the bhakta receives prema-bhakti. Sri Radha is the supreme personification of this Ahladini shakti, and therefore she is the source of Krishna's own bliss as much as Krishna is the source of Radha's bliss. As verse 7 of the Radha Stotram declares: Ahladini-shakti-rupe Krishna-prema-svarupini — 'You are of the form of the Ahladini shakti, the very embodiment of Krishna-prema.' (Sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana; six Goswamis of Vrindavan.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Vrishabhanu and what is the significance of Barsana?
seoFaqs.answer: Vrishabhanu Maharaja was the prosperous king-cowherd of the village of Barsana in the Vraja-mandala, husband of Kirtida (also called Kirtida-rani), and through divine arrangement the father of Sri Radha — who appeared to him not from his wife's womb but as a baby found floating on a lotus on the lake at Ravala (or in some traditions adopted by him after Yogamaya placed her there). Barsana, situated about 50 km north-west of Mathura on the Mathura-Vrindavan-Govardhan parikrama route, is the eternal home of Sri Radha-Rani and is therefore called Barsaneshvari Dham. The famous Shri Radharani temple atop the Brahma-giri hill at Barsana, the four hills of Brahma-giri/Vishnu-giri/Mahadev-giri/Sankarshana-giri representing the four Vedas, the spectacular Lath-mar Holi celebrated only here, and the Radha-Ashtami festival on Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami all centre on Sri Radha's appearance at Barsana. (Sources: Garga Samhita; classical Vrindavan parikrama tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Radha Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Radha Stotram is most powerful when recited at Brahma muhurta or in the sandhya hours, after bath, before a Sri Radha-Krishna or Sri Radharani murti, on a clean asana, with deep contemplation on the divine form of Radha described in the dhyana and verses. It is especially auspicious on Wednesdays (Budhvar — sacred to Krishna and Radha), Fridays (Shukravar — sacred to Devi), every Ekadashi, on Radha Ashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami — Radha's appearance day, the supreme parayana day at Barsana), Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami), Sharad Purnima (the maha-rasa night), Holi (Krishna's lila), Govardhana Puja, Akshaya Tritiya, and throughout Chaturmasya. The phalashruti within the stotra promises Krishna-prema and the supreme pada (abode) of Radha-Madhava for one who recites it bhakti-samyutah (united with devotion). (Sources: stotra phalashruti; Vaishnava recitation paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Radha Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti within the Radha Stotram itself promises: Radha-stotram-idam punyam yah pathed bhakti-samyutah, Krishna-premam labhet so'pi Radha-Madhavayoh padam — 'one who recites this sacred Radha Stotram united with devotion attains Krishna-prema and the supreme abode of Radha-Madhava.' Traditional Vaishnava acharyas additionally attribute the following benefits: deepening of pure prema-bhakti (divine love) for the divine couple Radha-Krishna; awakening of the latent ahladini shakti within the bhakta's heart through Radha's grace; protection from material desires and worldly attachments through immersion in Radha's prema; entry into the eight-fold daily lilas (ashta-kala-lila) of the divine couple in Vraja and Goloka; suitability for sakhi-bhava sadhana — the path of becoming a serving sakhi of Sri Radha; and ultimately nitya-vasa in Goloka with eternal seva of Sri Radha-Madhava. (Sources: stotra phalashruti; Gaudiya bhakti tradition.)
